data.title: Stellar (XLM) claims its protocol ensures better accountability for institutions than PoS networks, with $650M tokenized assets backing its argument.
data.description: Stellar (XLM) is highlighting the advantages of its consensus protocol in providing regulated issuers with improved accountability compared to PoS networks. The platform cites the tokenization of assets worth $650 million as evidence of this validation.

data.title: Brazos Midstream expands Midland Basin system with 300 MMcf/d cryogenic plant, largest built by the company.
data.description: Brazos Midstream, based in Fort Worth, Texas, has revealed the commissioning of Sundance II, a new cryogenic natural gas processing facility capable of handling 300 million cubic feet per day (MMcf/d), making it the largest plant of its kind built by the company so far. This facility, along with Sundance I which handles 200 MMcf/d and commenced operations in mid-2024, forms the Sundance processing complex, enhancing the company's processing capacity in the heart of the Midland Basin. This strategic move positions Brazos well for ongoing growth and operations in the region.

data.title: Wingstop stock surged 12% on earnings beat despite revenue miss. Short interest at 17%, $175.69M sales, $1.3B global sales, 124 net new restaurants. Domestic same-store sales down 5.8%.
data.description: Wingstop Inc. saw a significant surge in its stock price, rising by over 12% on Wednesday, following the release of its earnings report. Despite a slight miss on quarterly revenue, the company outperformed profit expectations. The fourth-quarter saw Wingstop achieve adjusted earnings per share of $1, surpassing the anticipated 84 cents. Quarterly sales totaled $175.69 million, slightly below the expected $177.533 million. In the same period, system-wide sales grew by 9.3% from 2024 to reach $1.3 billion. Moreover, the company successfully opened 124 new restaurants, with a domestic restaurant AUV of $2.0 million. However, domestic same-store sales experienced a 5.8% decline compared to the previous year. Notably, digital orders contributed to 73.2% of total sales. Furthermore, short interest, already high at over 17% of the float, likely played a role in boosting buying pressure.

data.title: Crypto venture capital firm, Dragonfly Capital, raises $650M for fourth fund, despite market challenges.
data.description: Dragonfly Capital, a prominent crypto venture capital company, has recently finished raising funds for its fourth fund. The specialized venture capital fund, with a focus on blockchain technology, managed to raise a substantial $650 million. This achievement stands out as one of the largest fundraising efforts within the sector according to a recent Coindesk article. Haseeb Qureshi, the managing partner at Dragonfly, expressed mixed emotions about the success of the fund, acknowledging the challenging market conditions and prevailing fear in the industry. Despite the difficulties, Qureshi highlighted the firm's history of raising funds during bear markets, citing the success of Fund I in 2018. For additional details, the full article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Insulet Corp. upsurges with Q4 earnings beating $1.55 per share, sales of $783.8M (31.2% YoY), total Omnipod revenue $781.8M (33.5%).
data.description: Insulet Corporation's (NASDAQ: PODD) stock is experiencing an increase following the release of its fourth-quarter earnings report and 2026 guidance. The company exceeded expectations with fourth-quarter adjusted earnings of $1.55 per share, surpassing the consensus of $1.45. Sales for the quarter reached $783.8 million, marking a 31.2% increase year over year or 29% in constant currency (CC), exceeding the consensus of $768.69 million. Total Omnipod revenue amounted to $781.8 million, reflecting a 33.5% increase or 31.3% in constant currency. Additionally, Drug Delivery revenue was reported at $2.0 million. The company achieved a gross margin of 72.5%, up 40 basis points from the previous year. Operating income reached $146.3 million, accounting for 18.7% of revenue and marking a 40-basis point increase from the prior year. Adjusted operating income was at $146.2 million.

data.title: Northborne Partners advises FORTA's acquisition by The Heritage Group. Deal announced on Feb. 18, 2026. No financial terms disclosed.
data.description: Northborne Partners, a financial advisory firm based in Minneapolis, revealed on February 18, 2026, that it assisted FORTA, a company owned by Riverarch Equity Partners, in its acquisition by The Heritage Group. The specific financial details of the transaction were not disclosed.
